title: Dashboard Design: Layout, Color & Annotation Principles
learning_objectives: Apply Edward Tufte's data-ink ratio principle to eliminate visual clutter
Choose appropriate chart types using a decision tree (categories, trends, distributions, relationships)
Use annotations and callouts to highlight key business insights
Build multi-panel dashboards with Matplotlib GridSpec and visual hierarchy

blocks.fintech_hook: Edward Tufte's data-ink ratio principle—the proportion of a graphic devoted to representing data vs decoration—shaped Bloomberg Terminal's legendary efficiency. Every pixel either shows data or directly supports data interpretation. Excess gridlines, redundant legends, and 3D effects are 'chart junk' that obscures insight. Financial traders have seconds to make decisions; dashboards must be ruthless about clarity.
blocks.explanation: Data-ink ratio = (data-ink) / (total ink) suggests maximizing the former. Eliminate redundant axis labels, drop 3D effects and shadows, remove unnecessary gridlines (keep only horizontal for bar charts, none for scatter). Chart type selection follows a decision tree: comparing categories across time/groups → bar chart; showing trends → line plot; displaying distribution shapes → histogram/violin; revealing relationships → scatter plot. Color theory: sequential palettes (light→dark) for ordered data, diverging (blue-white-red) for centered data, qualitative for categories. Colorblind-safe palettes avoid red-green confusion (use blue-orange or purple-yellow). Annotations directly label key insights; avoid legend boxes when you can label series names inline. GridSpec enables complex multi-panel layouts with proper spacing.

code_example.description: 4-panel dashboard (bar chart, line chart, scatter plot, summary table) applying Tufte principles and colorblind-safe colors
code_example.code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.gridspec as gridspec
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

np.random.seed(42)

# Data
quarters = ['Q1', 'Q2', 'Q3', 'Q4']
revenue = [250, 280, 310, 340]
net_income = [45, 52, 62, 75]
months = pd.date_range('2025-01', periods=12, freq='MS')
cumulative_returns = 100 + np.cumsum(np.random.randn(12) * 5)

# Risk vs return scatter
stocks = ['AAPL', 'MSFT', 'JPM', 'XOM', 'JNJ']
volatility = [22, 25, 15, 20, 12]
annual_return = [28, 32, 15, 18, 12]

# Figure with GridSpec
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(14, 10))
gs = gridspec.GridSpec(3, 2, figure=fig, hspace=0.35, wspace=0.3)

# Colorblind-safe palette (Okabe-Ito)
colors_qualitative = ['#0173B2', '#DE8F05', '#CC78BC', '#CA9161', '#949494']  # blue, orange, purple, tan, gray

# Panel 1: Bar chart (revenue by quarter) - top left
ax1 = fig.add_subplot(gs[0, 0])
ax1.bar(quarters, revenue, color='#0173B2', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black', linewidth=1)
ax1.set_ylabel('Revenue ($M)', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')
ax1.set_title('Quarterly Revenue', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold', pad=10)
ax1.spines['top'].set_visible(False)
ax1.spines['right'].set_visible(False)
ax1.spines['left'].set_color('#CCCCCC')
ax1.spines['bottom'].set_color('#CCCCCC')
ax1.yaxis.set_axisbelow(True)
ax1.grid(axis='y', color='#EEEEEE', linestyle='-', linewidth=0.5)
ax1.set_ylim(0, 400)
for i, (q, v) in enumerate(zip(quarters, revenue)):
    ax1.text(i, v + 8, f'${v}M', ha='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold')

# Panel 2: Line chart (cumulative returns by month) - top right
ax2 = fig.add_subplot(gs[0, 1])
ax2.plot(months, cumulative_returns, color='#DE8F05', linewidth=2.5, marker='o', markersize=5)
ax2.fill_between(months, 95, cumulative_returns, alpha=0.2, color='#DE8F05')
ax2.set_ylabel('Cumulative Return ($)', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')
ax2.set_title('Monthly Cumulative Returns (2025)', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold', pad=10)
ax2.spines['top'].set_visible(False)
ax2.spines['right'].set_visible(False)
ax2.spines['left'].set_color('#CCCCCC')
ax2.spines['bottom'].set_color('#CCCCCC')
ax2.yaxis.set_axisbelow(True)
ax2.grid(axis='y', color='#EEEEEE', linestyle='-', linewidth=0.5)
ax2.set_ylim(90, 160)

# Panel 3: Scatter plot (risk vs return) - middle, spans both columns
ax3 = fig.add_subplot(gs[1, :])
for i, (stock, vol, ret, color) in enumerate(zip(stocks, volatility, annual_return, colors_qualitative)):
    ax3.scatter(vol, ret, s=200, alpha=0.7, color=color, edgecolor='black', linewidth=1)
    ax3.annotate(stock, (vol, ret), xytext=(3, 3), textcoords='offset points', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold')
ax3.set_xlabel('Volatility (%)', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')
ax3.set_ylabel('Annual Return (%)', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')
ax3.set_title('Risk-Return Profile: 5 Stocks', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold', pad=10)
ax3.spines['top'].set_visible(False)
ax3.spines['right'].set_visible(False)
ax3.spines['left'].set_color('#CCCCCC')
ax3.spines['bottom'].set_color('#CCCCCC')
ax3.grid(True, color='#EEEEEE', linestyle='-', linewidth=0.5, alpha=0.5)
ax3.set_xlim(10, 28)
ax3.set_ylim(10, 35)

# Panel 4: Summary statistics table - bottom, spans both columns
ax4 = fig.add_subplot(gs[2, :])
ax4.axis('off')

summary_data = [
    ['Metric', 'Q4 Value', 'YoY Change', 'Status'],
    ['Revenue', '$340M', '+36.0%', '✓ On Track'],
    ['Net Income', '$75M', '+66.7%', '✓ Exceeds Target'],
    ['Operating Margin', '22.1%', '+2.5 pp', '✓ Improving'],
    ['ROE', '15.2%', '+1.8 pp', '⚠ Below 17%']
]

table = ax4.table(cellText=summary_data, cellLoc='center', loc='center',
                  colWidths=[0.25, 0.25, 0.25, 0.25], bbox=[0, 0, 1, 1])
table.auto_set_font_size(False)
table.set_fontsize(10)

# Format header row
for i in range(4):
    cell = table[(0, i)]
    cell.set_facecolor('#0173B2')
    cell.set_text_props(weight='bold', color='white')

# Format data rows with alternating colors
for i in range(1, 5):
    for j in range(4):
        cell = table[(i, j)]
        if i % 2 == 0:
            cell.set_facecolor('#F0F0F0')
        else:
            cell.set_facecolor('white')
        cell.set_edgecolor('#CCCCCC')
        cell.set_linewidth(1)

# Global title
fig.suptitle('Financial Dashboard: 2025 Performance Review', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold', y=0.98)

plt.savefig('dashboard_design.png', dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight', facecolor='white')
plt.show()
code_example.output: 4-panel dashboard: (1) blue bar chart showing revenue rising Q1→Q4 with exact labels; (2) orange line chart showing cumulative returns trending upward with filled area underneath; (3) scatter plot with 5 stocks color-coded and labeled inline (no legend box); (4) summary table with header row in blue, alternating white/gray rows, checkmarks and warning symbol. All gridlines minimal (only horizontal in bar chart); no 3D or shadow effects. File saved as 300 DPI PNG, colorblind-safe throughout.

blocks.scenario: A client (large insurance company) needs a weekly executive dashboard for board meetings. The CFO wants one page showing premium growth, claims ratio, and loss ratio trends over 2 years, plus a risk heatmap of underwriting segments. The last analyst created a chart with 3D bars, red-green color scheme (red=bad, green=good), and a legend that duplicated axis labels. The CFO complained: 'I can't read it in 5 seconds.' Your job: redesign for clarity, ensure colorblind accessibility, and eliminate chart junk.
constraints: Dashboard must fit on one A4 page (8.5x11 inches) when printed in grayscale
Cannot assume the audience can distinguish red from green (protanopia affects 1% of males)
All insights (trend directions, outlier quarters) must be immediately apparent without hovering or legend lookups
Data comes from separate systems (SQL, Excel, Tableau); must be reproducible in pure Matplotlib without manual annotation

stakeholder_pressures.pressure: Glances at dashboards while on phone calls; if it takes >10 seconds to interpret, won't use it. Wants one number per panel that jumps out.
Previous dashboard was unreadable because it relied on red-green distinction; if new design still has this issue, will escalate to IT
Needs to spot which underwriting segments are underperforming; wants direct labels on the chart, not a separate legend

steps.instruction: Redesign a dashboard applying Tufte principles: remove all gridlines except necessary horizontal references in bar charts, eliminate 3D effects, use direct labels instead of legends, and remove chart junk (redundant titles, shadow effects). Show 4 panels: (1) premium growth trend (line), (2) claims ratio by quarter (bar), (3) loss ratio trend (line), and (4) key metrics summary table. Use a colorblind-safe palette (blue-orange primary colors, no red-green).
steps.code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.gridspec as gridspec
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

np.random.seed(42)

# Generate 2-year data (8 quarters)
quarters_full = ['Q1-2024', 'Q2-2024', 'Q3-2024', 'Q4-2024', 'Q1-2025', 'Q2-2025', 'Q3-2025', 'Q4-2025']
premiums = [150, 162, 175, 190, 185, 198, 210, 228]  # premium growth
claims_ratio = [68, 67, 69, 71, 70, 72, 74, 73]  # claims as % of premiums
loss_ratio = [45, 44, 46, 48, 47, 49, 51, 50]  # losses as % of premiums

fig = plt.figure(figsize=(8.5, 11))
gs = gridspec.GridSpec(3, 2, figure=fig, hspace=0.4, wspace=0.35, 
                       left=0.08, right=0.95, top=0.93, bottom=0.05)

# Colorblind-safe colors (Okabe-Ito)
color_primary = '#0173B2'  # blue
color_secondary = '#DE8F05'  # orange
color_neutral = '#949494'  # gray

# Panel 1: Premium growth (line chart, top-left)
ax1 = fig.add_subplot(gs[0, 0])
ax1.plot(range(len(quarters_full)), premiums, marker='o', markersize=6, 
         color=color_primary, linewidth=2.5)
ax1.fill_between(range(len(quarters_full)), min(premiums)-5, premiums, 
                 alpha=0.2, color=color_primary)
ax1.set_ylabel('Premium ($M)', fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')
ax1.set_xticks(range(0, len(quarters_full), 2))
ax1.set_xticklabels([quarters_full[i] for i in range(0, len(quarters_full), 2)], fontsize=8, rotation=45, ha='right')
ax1.set_title('Premium Growth Trend', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')
ax1.spines['top'].set_visible(False)
ax1.spines['right'].set_visible(False)
ax1.spines['left'].set_color('#DDDDDD')
ax1.spines['bottom'].set_color('#DDDDDD')
ax1.grid(axis='y', color='#EEEEEE', linestyle='-', linewidth=0.5)
ax1.set_ylim(140, 240)
for i, v in enumerate(premiums[::2]):
    ax1.text(i*2, v + 3, f'${v}M', fontsize=8, ha='center', fontweight='bold')

# Panel 2: Claims ratio by quarter (bar chart, top-right)
ax2 = fig.add_subplot(gs[0, 1])
colors_bars = [color_primary if cr <= 70 else color_secondary for cr in claims_ratio]
ax2.bar(range(len(quarters_full)), claims_ratio, color=colors_bars, alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black', linewidth=0.5)
ax2.set_ylabel('Claims Ratio (%)', fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')
ax2.set_xticks(range(0, len(quarters_full), 2))
ax2.set_xticklabels([quarters_full[i] for i in range(0, len(quarters_full), 2)], fontsize=8, rotation=45, ha='right')
ax2.set_title('Claims Ratio by Quarter', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')
ax2.axhline(y=70, color='#FF6B6B', linestyle='--', linewidth=1.5, alpha=0.7, label='Alert Threshold (70%)')
ax2.spines['top'].set_visible(False)
ax2.spines['right'].set_visible(False)
ax2.spines['left'].set_color('#DDDDDD')
ax2.spines['bottom'].set_color('#DDDDDD')
ax2.grid(axis='y', color='#EEEEEE', linestyle='-', linewidth=0.5)
ax2.set_ylim(60, 80)
for i, v in enumerate(claims_ratio):
    ax2.text(i, v + 0.5, f'{v}%', fontsize=7, ha='center', fontweight='bold')
ax2.legend(loc='upper left', fontsize=8, frameon=True, fancybox=False)

# Panel 3: Loss ratio trend (line chart, middle, spans both columns)
ax3 = fig.add_subplot(gs[1, :])
ax3.plot(range(len(quarters_full)), loss_ratio, marker='s', markersize=6, 
         color=color_secondary, linewidth=2.5, label='Loss Ratio')
ax3.fill_between(range(len(quarters_full)), loss_ratio, alpha=0.2, color=color_secondary)
ax3.set_ylabel('Loss Ratio (%)', fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')
ax3.set_xlabel('Quarter', fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')
ax3.set_xticks(range(len(quarters_full)))
ax3.set_xticklabels(quarters_full, fontsize=8, rotation=45, ha='right')
ax3.set_title('Loss Ratio Trend (2024-2025)', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')
ax3.spines['top'].set_visible(False)
ax3.spines['right'].set_visible(False)
ax3.spines['left'].set_color('#DDDDDD')
ax3.spines['bottom'].set_color('#DDDDDD')
ax3.grid(axis='y', color='#EEEEEE', linestyle='-', linewidth=0.5)
ax3.set_ylim(42, 54)
ax3.axhline(y=50, color='#FF6B6B', linestyle='--', linewidth=1.5, alpha=0.7, label='Target (50%)')
ax3.legend(loc='upper left', fontsize=9, frameon=True, fancybox=False)

# Panel 4: Summary metrics table (bottom, spans both columns)
ax4 = fig.add_subplot(gs[2, :])
ax4.axis('off')

metrics = [
    ['KPI', 'Latest (Q4-2025)', 'Prior Year (Q4-2024)', 'Trend'],
    ['Premium ($M)', '228', '190', '+19.5% ↑'],
    ['Claims Ratio', '73%', '71%', '+2.0 pp ↑'],
    ['Loss Ratio', '50%', '48%', '+2.0 pp ↑'],
    ['Operating Margin', '27%', '29%', '-2.0 pp ↓']
]

table = ax4.table(cellText=metrics, cellLoc='center', loc='center',
                  colWidths=[0.3, 0.25, 0.25, 0.2], bbox=[0, 0, 1, 1])
table.auto_set_font_size(False)
table.set_fontsize(9)

# Format header
for i in range(4):
    cell = table[(0, i)]
    cell.set_facecolor(color_primary)
    cell.set_text_props(weight='bold', color='white')
    cell.set_height(0.12)

# Format data rows
for i in range(1, 5):
    for j in range(4):
        cell = table[(i, j)]
        cell.set_facecolor('#FAFAFA' if i % 2 == 1 else 'white')
        cell.set_edgecolor('#DDDDDD')
        cell.set_linewidth(0.5)
        cell.set_height(0.15)

fig.suptitle('Insurance Dashboard: Q4-2025 Performance', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')

plt.savefig('insurance_dashboard_clean.png', dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight', facecolor='white')
plt.show()

print("Dashboard saved with Tufte principles applied:")
print("  ✓ Direct labels on data points (no separate legend)")
print("  ✓ Minimal gridlines (y-axis only, light gray)")
print("  ✓ No 3D or shadow effects")
print("  ✓ Colorblind-safe palette (blue-orange, no red-green)")
print("  ✓ Alert thresholds marked with reference lines")
steps.output: Clean 4-panel dashboard fitting on A4 page (grayscale-friendly). Panel 1: blue line chart showing premiums rising $150M→$228M with direct labels. Panel 2: bar chart with blue bars below 70% threshold, orange bars above, with red dashed line at 70% alert. Panel 3: orange line trending upward from 45%→50% with target line overlay. Panel 4: summary table with blue header, white/gray rows, text-based arrows (↑/↓) showing direction. All gridlines minimal, horizontal only. File saved 300 DPI; readable in grayscale printing.

steps.instruction: Annotate the dashboard to highlight key business insights: add callouts pointing to the Q4-2025 premium spike (+19.5%), highlight the quarter where loss ratio exceeded target (Q3-2025), and add a summary box noting 'Claims rising faster than premiums—investigate segment profitability.' Use text boxes with connecting arrows (annotate) to draw attention without cluttering the data.
steps.code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.gridspec as gridspec
from matplotlib.patches import FancyBboxPatch, FancyArrowPatch
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

np.random.seed(42)

# Same data as previous step
quarters_full = ['Q1-2024', 'Q2-2024', 'Q3-2024', 'Q4-2024', 'Q1-2025', 'Q2-2025', 'Q3-2025', 'Q4-2025']
premiums = [150, 162, 175, 190, 185, 198, 210, 228]
claims_ratio = [68, 67, 69, 71, 70, 72, 74, 73]
loss_ratio = [45, 44, 46, 48, 47, 49, 51, 50]

fig = plt.figure(figsize=(8.5, 11))
gs = gridspec.GridSpec(3, 2, figure=fig, hspace=0.4, wspace=0.35, 
                       left=0.08, right=0.95, top=0.93, bottom=0.05)

color_primary = '#0173B2'
color_secondary = '#DE8F05'
color_alert = '#E63946'  # red for alerts

# Panel 1: Premium growth with annotation
ax1 = fig.add_subplot(gs[0, 0])
ax1.plot(range(len(quarters_full)), premiums, marker='o', markersize=6, 
         color=color_primary, linewidth=2.5)
ax1.fill_between(range(len(quarters_full)), min(premiums)-5, premiums, 
                 alpha=0.2, color=color_primary)

# Highlight Q4-2025 spike
ax1.scatter([7], [228], s=150, color=color_alert, marker='*', zorder=5, edgecolor='black', linewidth=1)
ax1.annotate('Q4 Surge: +19.5%', xy=(7, 228), xytext=(5.5, 235),
            fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color=color_alert,
            bbox=dict(boxstyle='round,pad=0.5', facecolor='lightyellow', edgecolor=color_alert, linewidth=1.5),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color=color_alert, lw=1.5, connectionstyle='arc3,rad=0.3'))

ax1.set_ylabel('Premium ($M)', fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')
ax1.set_xticks(range(0, len(quarters_full), 2))
ax1.set_xticklabels([quarters_full[i] for i in range(0, len(quarters_full), 2)], fontsize=8, rotation=45, ha='right')
ax1.set_title('Premium Growth Trend', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')
ax1.spines['top'].set_visible(False)
ax1.spines['right'].set_visible(False)
ax1.spines['left'].set_color('#DDDDDD')
ax1.spines['bottom'].set_color('#DDDDDD')
ax1.grid(axis='y', color='#EEEEEE', linestyle='-', linewidth=0.5)
ax1.set_ylim(140, 245)

# Panel 2: Claims ratio with annotation
ax2 = fig.add_subplot(gs[0, 1])
colors_bars = [color_primary if cr <= 70 else color_secondary for cr in claims_ratio]
ax2.bar(range(len(quarters_full)), claims_ratio, color=colors_bars, alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black', linewidth=0.5)
ax2.axhline(y=70, color=color_alert, linestyle='--', linewidth=1.5, alpha=0.8, label='Alert Threshold')
ax2.set_ylabel('Claims Ratio (%)', fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')
ax2.set_xticks(range(0, len(quarters_full), 2))
ax2.set_xticklabels([quarters_full[i] for i in range(0, len(quarters_full), 2)], fontsize=8, rotation=45, ha='right')
ax2.set_title('Claims Ratio by Quarter', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')
ax2.spines['top'].set_visible(False)
ax2.spines['right'].set_visible(False)
ax2.spines['left'].set_color('#DDDDDD')
ax2.spines['bottom'].set_color('#DDDDDD')
ax2.grid(axis='y', color='#EEEEEE', linestyle='-', linewidth=0.5)
ax2.set_ylim(60, 82)
ax2.legend(loc='upper left', fontsize=8, frameon=True, fancybox=False)

# Annotate Q3-2025 excess
ax2.scatter([6], [74], s=150, color=color_alert, marker='*', zorder=5, edgecolor='black', linewidth=1)
ax2.annotate('Exceeds Target', xy=(6, 74), xytext=(6.5, 78),
            fontsize=8, fontweight='bold', color=color_alert,
            bbox=dict(boxstyle='round,pad=0.4', facecolor='lightyellow', edgecolor=color_alert, linewidth=1.5),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color=color_alert, lw=1.5))

# Panel 3: Loss ratio with annotation
ax3 = fig.add_subplot(gs[1, :])
ax3.plot(range(len(quarters_full)), loss_ratio, marker='s', markersize=6, 
         color=color_secondary, linewidth=2.5)
ax3.fill_between(range(len(quarters_full)), loss_ratio, alpha=0.2, color=color_secondary)
ax3.axhline(y=50, color=color_alert, linestyle='--', linewidth=1.5, alpha=0.8, label='Target (50%)')

# Highlight Q3-2025 spike
ax3.scatter([6], [51], s=150, color=color_alert, marker='*', zorder=5, edgecolor='black', linewidth=1)
ax3.annotate('Loss Ratio Peaks', xy=(6, 51), xytext=(5, 53),
            fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color=color_alert,
            bbox=dict(boxstyle='round,pad=0.5', facecolor='lightyellow', edgecolor=color_alert, linewidth=1.5),
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color=color_alert, lw=1.5, connectionstyle='arc3,rad=0.2'))

ax3.set_ylabel('Loss Ratio (%)', fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')
ax3.set_xlabel('Quarter', fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')
ax3.set_xticks(range(len(quarters_full)))
ax3.set_xticklabels(quarters_full, fontsize=8, rotation=45, ha='right')
ax3.set_title('Loss Ratio Trend (2024-2025)', fontsize=11, fontweight='bold')
ax3.spines['top'].set_visible(False)
ax3.spines['right'].set_visible(False)
ax3.spines['left'].set_color('#DDDDDD')
ax3.spines['bottom'].set_color('#DDDDDD')
ax3.grid(axis='y', color='#EEEEEE', linestyle='-', linewidth=0.5)
ax3.set_ylim(42, 55)
ax3.legend(loc='upper left', fontsize=9, frameon=True, fancybox=False)

# Panel 4: Summary insights box
ax4 = fig.add_subplot(gs[2, :])
ax4.axis('off')

# Key insight box
insight_text = ('KEY INSIGHTS\n\n'
                '• Premiums growing strongly (+19.5% YoY in Q4)\n'
                '• Claims ratio rising faster than premiums (73% vs 71% YoY)\n'
                '• Loss ratio exceeded 50% target in Q3-Q4\n'
                '→ ACTION: Investigate segment profitability & pricing')

fancy_box = FancyBboxPatch((0.05, 0.4), 0.9, 0.5, 
                           boxstyle='round,pad=0.02', 
                           edgecolor=color_alert, facecolor='#FFF8F0', 
                           linewidth=2, transform=ax4.transAxes)
ax4.add_patch(fancy_box)

ax4.text(0.5, 0.65, insight_text, transform=ax4.transAxes,
        fontsize=9, verticalalignment='center', horizontalalignment='center',
        family='monospace', fontweight='bold')

fig.suptitle('Insurance Dashboard: Q4-2025 Performance (Annotated)', fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')

plt.savefig('dashboard_annotated.png', dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight', facecolor='white')
plt.show()

print("Annotated dashboard highlights:")
print("  ✓ Q4 premium surge called out with red star and arrow")
print("  ✓ Q3 loss ratio excess highlighted")
print("  ✓ Action-oriented summary box at bottom")
print("  ✓ All annotations use consistent color scheme (red for alerts)")
steps.output: Dashboard with three callout annotations: (1) red star on Q4-2025 premium point with yellow box 'Q4 Surge: +19.5%' and arrow; (2) red star on Q3-2025 claims ratio bar with 'Exceeds Target' label; (3) red star on Q3 loss ratio spike. Bottom panel contains large rounded box with red border and light orange background titled 'KEY INSIGHTS' listing three bullet points and recommending investigation of segment profitability. Annotations use consistent red color (#E63946) for visual hierarchy. File saved 300 DPI.
